_comment: Track 4 evaluation corpus. Each task pairs a natural-language intent with a program (JSON authoring surface or canonical .aury) and optional concrete oracle checks. `aury eval eval/corpus.json` runs the closed repair loop over all tasks and prints a repair-convergence table. Program paths are relative to this manifest. expect_accept:false marks a deliberately-wrong spec whose correct outcome is a rejection.

tasks.name: gcd
tasks.intent: Euclid's GCD of two non-negative integers; commutative, and divides both inputs.

tasks.name: loop-factorial
tasks.intent: Iterative factorial with a mutable accumulator (Track 2 loops); requires 0<=n<=20, ensures result>=1.

tasks.name: mean-f64
tasks.intent: Mean of a non-empty vector of f64 (Track 3 floats + Track 2 loops); guarded against the empty vector.

tasks.name: parse-classify
tasks.intent: Parse a string to i64, falling back to a default when it is not a number.

tasks.name: dice-effect
tasks.intent: Draw a random even integer using the rng effect inside a region (effects + regions).

tasks.name: effect-leak
tasks.intent: Uses the rng effect without declaring it; the loop should mechanically widen the effect row and accept.

tasks.name: vec-pipeline
tasks.intent: map/filter/reduce over a growable (vec f64) via vec-push (Track B): scale, keep-above, sum.

tasks.name: alias-region
tasks.intent: Two mut references share a region (may alias); the loop should split the region and accept.

tasks.name: vec-use-after-move
tasks.intent: A vector is pushed then reused (use-after-move); the loop should insert a copy and accept.

tasks.name: log-scope
tasks.intent: Uses the scoped `log` capability without a grant; the loop should wrap it in a (with (log) ...) scope and accept — a capability-scope convergence distinct from widening an effect row.

tasks.name: clock-scope
tasks.intent: Reads the scoped `clock` capability without a grant; the loop should wrap the read in a (with (clock) ...) scope and accept. clock.now is a deterministic tick (0 on first read), so the oracle is stable.

tasks.name: calculator
tasks.intent: A small calculator library (add, multiply, factorial, gcd, fib, ...) with properties.

tasks.name: unterminated
tasks.intent: Correct program with a missing closing parenthesis — the loop's parse repair should close it.

tasks.name: false-property
tasks.intent: A DELIBERATELY WRONG spec: claims a+b > a for all i64 (false when b<=0). The loop must correctly refuse to accept.
